sql_array: CREATE TABLE prices (id INT, value DECIMAL(10,2), type VARCHAR(50), price1 DECIMAL(10,2), price2 DECIMAL(10,2), price3 DECIMAL(10,2));
INSERT INTO prices (id, value, type, price1, price2, price3) VALUES (1, 10.50, 'A', NULL, 20.00, NULL);
INSERT INTO prices (id, value, type, price1, price2, price3) VALUES (2, 15.75, 'B', 5.00, 15.00, 30.00);
INSERT INTO prices (id, value, type, price1, price2, price3) VALUES (3, NULL, 'C', 0.00, 22.50, 45.00);
INSERT INTO prices (id, value, type, price1, price2, price3) VALUES (4, 20.00, 'A', 3.50, NULL, 12.50);
SELECT __min_notnull(value, (SELECT MAX(price1) FROM prices WHERE price2 IS NOT NULL)) AS min_value FROM prices WHERE type = 'A';

SELECT p1.id, p1.value, __min_notnull(p1.price1, p2.price2) AS min_price FROM prices p1 JOIN prices p2 ON p1.id <> p2.id WHERE p1.value IS NOT NULL GROUP BY p1.id, p1.value HAVING __min_notnull(p1.price1, p2.price2) > 0;
SELECT id, (SELECT __min_notnull(price1, price2, price3) FROM prices WHERE id = p.id) AS min_price FROM prices p WHERE __min_notnull(price1, price2, price3) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT a.id, a.value, __min_notnull(a.price1, (SELECT MAX(b.price2) FROM prices b WHERE b.type = a.type)) AS min_compared_price FROM prices a GROUP BY a.id, a.value ORDER BY min_compared_price DESC;

WITH MinPrices AS (SELECT type, __min_notnull(price1, price2, price3) AS min_price FROM prices GROUP BY type) SELECT p.id, p.type, p.value, mp.min_price FROM prices p JOIN MinPrices mp ON p.type = mp.type WHERE mp.min_price < 10;
